title: silene nutans subsp. glabra
intro: Silene nutans subsp. glabra, also known as white campion or rough catchfly, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its antitussive and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Silene nutans subsp. glabra are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
